name: Cornac (Comparative Framework for Multimodal Recommender Systems)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Preferred.AI / Singapore Management University

performance.notes: Cython-optimized; GPU via PyTorch for deep models; efficient for research benchmarking

agent_readiness.notes: Active multimodal recommender system research framework. import cornac. from cornac.models import MF, BPR, VAECF, BiVAECF, EASE. data = cornac.datasets.movielens.load_feedback(). rs = cornac.eval_methods.RatioSplit(data, test_size=0.2). mf = MF(k=10, max_iter=200, learning_rate=0.01). cornac.Experiment(eval_method=rs, models=[mf], metrics=[cornac.metrics.RMSE()]).run(). 70+ models: MF, SVD++, BPR, VAECF, C-VAE, NGCF, LightGCN, EASE, BiVAECF, MTER. Multimodal: text, image, graph side information. Active development — newer models added regularly.

gotchas: Research-focused — not designed for production deployment
70+ models make selection complex without background in RecSys literature
Some models require GPU for practical training time
Evaluation is batch-oriented — no online/streaming recommendation
Less documentation than Surprise for beginners
best_when: Research benchmarking across many recommendation algorithms
Multimodal recommendations using text/image/graph side information
Comparing collaborative filtering to graph-based and deep learning models
Academic papers requiring reproducible recommendation experiments
avoid_when: Simple prototypes (Surprise has better beginner ergonomics)
When only implicit feedback ALS is needed (use implicit library)
Teams not familiar with recommender systems literature
summary: Cornac is an active multimodal recommender systems research framework with 70+ algorithms. cornac.Experiment(eval_method=RatioSplit(data), models=[MF(), BPR(), VAECF()], metrics=[RMSE(), NDCG()]).run(). Supports multimodal side information (text, images, graphs). Models: MF, BPR, VAECF, LightGCN, NGCF, EASE, BiVAECF. Apache-2.0. Best for RecSys research and benchmarking. Not for production deployment.
